steps.title: Compare host dependence
steps.content: Three-host parasites declined as temperatures rose and depend on at least two vertebrate species. Two-host parasites remained stable and depend on only one vertebrate species, suggesting that warming affects parasite abundance through changes in required vertebrate hosts.

steps.title: Match the study pattern
steps.content: Choice A reverses which parasite was more affected. Choice B contradicts the point that marine vertebrates are vulnerable. Choice C explains declined three-host parasites and stable two-host parasites among the studied fish hosts. Choice D reverses the relationship between host number and sensitivity. The answer is C.
